supply_chain.dependencies: Reliance on third-party service providers for data center and cloud services, including Kyndryl for IT infrastructure and AWS for cloud services. Heavy dependence on USPS and third-party carriers for physical delivery of communications.
supply_chain.risks: Failure of third-party service providers to perform their functions could result in material interruptions. Single or limited number of service providers for certain businesses. USPS financial losses may lead to service disruptions. Carrier disruptions due to strikes, weather, or increased costs.

regulatory_risks: Changes in SEC rules and regulations affecting proxy materials distribution and stockholder communications
Changes in NYSE rules regarding reimbursement rates for proxy services
FINRA regulatory oversight of broker-dealer operations
DOL ERISA regulations on plan fiduciaries and retirement plan services
Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council (FFIEC) regulatory examination
Privacy and information security regulations including GLBA, HIPAA, CPRA, and GDPR
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) compliance
Anti-money laundering (AML) laws and Bank Secrecy Act compliance
OFAC sanctions and trade control laws
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and UK Bribery Act compliance
New or changing legal requirements related to AI use
State and foreign privacy laws with inconsistent requirements across jurisdictions
Increased focus on cybersecurity and vendor risk management by regulators
Colorado Division of Banking and Arizona Department of Insurance regulations for trust company operations

sanction_risks: Restrictions on transactions with specified countries and individuals on sanctions lists
Impact of international conflicts such as Russia-Ukraine war and Middle East conflicts on operations
Trade policies, disputes, barriers and restrictions affecting international operations
